meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: The Model Race Goes Nuclear: GPT-5.6 Sol, Grok 4.5, and Fable All Launch in the Same Week
stories.summary: OpenAI shipped its GPT-5.6 family (Sol/Terra/Luna) publicly the same week xAI launched Grok 4.5 as a cheap, fast Opus-class model and Anthropic extended free access to Fable 5. Builders spent the week comparing all three head-to-head, with Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 joining as a fourth contender.

takes.name: OpenAI
takes.text:
GPT-5.6 Sol, along with Terra and Luna, will launch publicly this Thursday.

We’re expanding preview access globally now. https://t.co/Uk5HcfSc2e

takes.name: Elon Musk
takes.text:
Based on strong positive feedback from customers in our beta test program, @SpaceXAI will make Grok 4.5 available to the public tomorrow.

It is an Opus-class model, but faster, more token-efficient and lower cost.

takes.name: Claude
takes.text: We're extending access to Claude Fable 5 on all paid plans through July 12.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
Apparently I'm allowed to talk about GPT-5.6 now?

It's a damn good model. Not quite as "smart" as Fable, but it is incredibly capable. Fixed all the problems I had with GPT-5.5.

It is incredibly determined. Will run for a day without even using a /goal. It understands subagents

takes.name: Michael Truell
takes.text:
Excited to release Grok 4.5 with @SpaceXAI. 

It's an Opus-class model that's fast and low cost. It's a significant step up over any model we've developed so far, including Composer 2.5, and has become the daily driver for many on our team. 

First of many releases. More soon.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
i've never hyped a model release, we're generally conservative with how we use these things

but gpt-5.6 has had a massive impact on our team, we're using 5x the tokens as we used to

it's not even smarter than fable or anything, but it's just so reliable and fun to use

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
“Fable is a 'wise owl' who is very thoughtful and very well spoken, GPT-5.6-Sol is like a rottweiler who will grab the problem by the throat and not let go until it is done.”

Best explanation I’ve heard so far.

takes.name: Ben Davis
takes.text:
On Fable being smarter than 5.6 - it is, but:

I think smart is the wrong word, the thing 5.6 is lacking behind fable is the "big model smell" which roughly translates to "understanding the unsaid intent behind your prompt"

fable fills in the blanks better, it writes more

takes.name: spidey
takes.text:
the last couple of days has been crazy 

> Anthropic brings back Fable 5
> OpenAI launched GPT 5.6
> Grok launched Grok 4.5
> Meta launched Meta Muse Spark 1.1

ai race is getting interesting https://t.co/U2KtYVb8cu

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: Been using Grok 4.5 for most of my tasks the last couple days and it's good. Not quite Fable level I think, but it's a great coding model and it's WAY faster and cheaper (especially factoring in all the thinking Fable does).

stories.title: Bun Gets Rewritten in Rust, and It Cost Basically Nothing in AI Tokens
stories.summary: Jarred Sumner announced Bun is being rewritten in Rust, and developers were floored that the whole effort cost roughly $165k in AI tokens versus the ~14 engineer-months it took to rewrite Turborepo in Rust the old-fashioned way.

takes.name: Jarred Sumner
takes.text:
Rewriting Bun in Rust

https://t.co/Rl8bcaxBFc

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: The Bun rewrite in Rust would have cost about $165k in tokens if it was done over API https://t.co/nD88V86Fjc

takes.name: Jared Palmer
takes.text: The Bun rewrite was dirt cheap even at Fable pricing. For comparison, it took 3-5 engineers (about half the core team) about ~14 months to rewrite @Turborepo in Rust between 2023 and 2024. When you consider the opportunity cost on top of eng salaries, it actually cost several

takes.name: dex
takes.text:
I love the part of the bun rewrite that shows what’s possible and pushes people to ask their models for more

I am concerned about the part where people try to do this for things that 

1) aren’t already designed/architected
2) don’t have such a comprehensive test suite and

takes.name: 图拉鼎
takes.text: 看了这篇 Bun 用 Rust 重写的文章，重点不是 Fable 多厉害，也不是 Rust 多好，而是在 AI 时代，全功能的编译型、全生命周期管理的语言（像 Rust 这样的），将会加速得到适配。Swift，你也要加油啊！

stories.title: Agent Skills Tooling Keeps Evolving: Skill Repos, Papercuts, and Subagent Forks
stories.summary: The "context engineering" toolkit for coding agents keeps maturing - Matt Pocock shipped skills v1.1 with new planning commands, while others shared small but sharp workflow hacks like subagent forking and a "papercuts" complaint log for agents.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
My skills repo has 160K stars, 7.5m downloads...

...and no tutorial.

So, here it is. Watch me walk through the essential skills:

- /grill-with-docs
- /to-spec
- /to-tickets
- /implement
- /code-review

It's the whole flow, end-to-end. Enjoy: https://t.co/mVdRQEjSMp

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
mattpocock/skills v1.1 is out!

- /wayfinder helps you plan more ambitious work than ever
- /to-spec and /to-tickets replace /to-prd and /to-issues
- /implement + /code-review complete the whole lifecycle
- /research and /prototype help support wayfinder, or can be used https://t.co/ibrxXXCDb5

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: I added a tiny "papercuts" cli tool that agents can use to complain about bullshit they encountered during work, like dead-end tool calls, broken links, or other frustrations. The models would usually just push through without mentioning any problems https://t.co/oLLRaV3cDm

takes.name: Delba
takes.text:
Smol tip: /𝚏𝚘𝚛𝚔 is perfect for mid-task side quests.

It spawns a subagent that inherits the whole conversation, runs in the background while you keep working on your main quest, and comes back with an update. https://t.co/EE4MS41sJZ

takes.name: dex
takes.text: @mattpocockuk T minus 3 weeks until you’re running around turning off every tool and subagent and 6 weeks until you give up trying to give up with the bloat and end up switching harnesses or writing your own

stories.title: AI Is Eating Design Systems: Extract, Generate, and Refine in One Prompt
stories.summary: A cluster of designers shared AI-powered design tooling this week - from extracting a full design system off any website to Apple WWDC-inspired motion principles and Figma shader experiments, showing how fast AI is absorbing design-system busywork.

takes.name: Emil Kowalski
takes.text:
New skill: /apple-design

Apple’s WWDC videos are a goldmine of knowledge. I’ve combed through my favorite ones and came up with 17 design and motion principles.

Use them to review existing work or when working on something new to get it right.

https://t.co/jCwhthpi20

takes.name: Abraham John 🦄🦓
takes.text:
UI/UX Designers, you need to see this.

I just discovered a tool called HyperDesign, and it’s one of the easiest ways to extract a complete design system from any website. Just paste a URL, and it generates a DESIGN.md file with the site’s colors, typography, spacing, and UI https://t.co/5SIO0ATBaJ

takes.name: Praveen Kumar
takes.text: More figma shaders magic https://t.co/CYtlnnJQPN

takes.name: rauno
takes.text:
this week @merycodes and I are refining the vercel design system by tweaking sizing, alignment, deleting redundancies... doing the "boring work"

why are we doing this? because we care :) https://t.co/OnSxh7LsNo

takes.name: Tom Johnson
takes.text:
legitimately impressive how @v0 can basically one-shot the actual Geist UI from a screenshot with the new design systems features.

It's graduated from:
"imagine you're in Vercel" to "wait this isn't Vercel?"

my workflow for internal prototypes is completely different now. https://t.co/DVI5ifmOxF

stories.title: Vercel Scoops Up Better Auth's Bekacru to Build Auth for Agents
stories.summary: Vercel's Guillermo Rauch welcomed Better Auth creator Bekacru to the team, framing it as a move to build open-source auth for both humans and AI agents. The dev community reacted with congrats and a few jokes about the inevitability of the deal.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
Welcome @bekacru to Vercel. It's a privilege to join forces with Bereket and Better Auth to further our Open SDK vision. 

Developers deserve better auth, to serve humans and agents, built in the open and with taste. That's what Bereket has accomplished. Congrats! 🇪🇹🇺🇸

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
Congrats to my bro @bekacru, if only you knew his story... a straight legend

Congrats to @rauchg @vercel this was a very smart move ♟️ https://t.co/GuMsHtXQ9K

takes.name: James Perkins
takes.text: This was inevitable! Congrats to the whole Better Auth team and Vercel.

takes.name: joel ⛈️
takes.text: @rauchg @bekacru hell yeah. love this. love better auth. what a great match.

stories.title: Devs Push Back on EU "Chat Control" and UK VPN Restrictions
stories.summary: European digital-rights rules are back in the crosshairs - developers slammed the EU's chat-control scanning proposal and new UK VPN restrictions as privacy overreach dressed up as safety regulation.

takes.name: DHH
takes.text: Chat control in the EU, VPN restrictions in the UK. All in service of ensuring that the natives don't dare critique the regimes online. When the Brits and the rest of Europe has finally had enough of this, it'll be an explosive moment.

takes.name: vaxry
takes.text:
They will shove this down everyone's throats until they succeed. I thought even the european tribunal shot this down once for being unconstitutional due to the proportionality rule?

ugh.

takes.name: vaxry
takes.text: to every politician who voted in favor of chat control, or against amendment 30, I wish you a very pleasant "your private messages get scanned, stored, then later hacked and leaked on the internet" with a side of "24/7 surveillance cameras in your house" and "gps tracker in your

stories.title: OpenAI Audits SWE-Bench Pro and Finds 30% of Tasks Are Broken
stories.summary: OpenAI published an audit of the widely-used SWE-Bench Pro coding benchmark, concluding it no longer reliably measures frontier coding capability - with roughly 30% of tasks found to be broken, prompting devs to question what benchmark gains even mean anymore.

takes.name: OpenAI
takes.text:
We audited SWE-Bench Pro, one of the most widely used AI coding benchmarks, and found it no longer reliably measures frontier coding capability.

We find 30% of SWE-Bench Pro tasks to be broken, and are retracting our previous recommendation that the research community use it as

takes.name: OpenAI
takes.text:
To audit SWE-Bench Pro, we used model-based investigator agents alongside independent reviews from five independent experienced software engineers.

That helped us examine tasks at scale while keeping expert judgment at the center. https://t.co/3PNbk57uvF

takes.name: ThePrimeagen
takes.text:
good

these benchmarks have been gamed for so long

Also, can we just take a moment and ask ourselves, wtf does 3% better SWE-Bench Pro even mean?  Does it mean ANYTHING at all to you?  No.  No it doesn't.  It just tickles your dumb lizard brain

stories.title: TypeScript 7 Is Officially Out
stories.summary: The TypeScript team shipped TypeScript 7, with a compatibility package for teams still needing the TS 6 API - developers are already installing it to try out the new release.

takes.name: TypeScript
takes.text:
📣 The moment is here. 📣

TypeScript 7 is officially released! 7️⃣

https://t.co/D7Mbq7vWpy

takes.name: TypeScript
takes.text:
We know people will still need TS 6 for API usage.

That's why there's a new `@typescript/typescript6` package that provides

- the TypeScript 6 API
- a separate `tsc6` binary

Install it side-by-side with TypeScript 7, and don't worry about conflicts with `tsc`.

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
https://t.co/ua6q1wswqc

TypeScript 7 here we go!

stories.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Live, Its Next-Gen Voice Mode
stories.summary: OpenAI rolled out GPT-Live, a new voice model for ChatGPT that Sam Altman says feels 'magical and real' enough to shift his own preference from typing to talking with AI.

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text:
GPT-live (next-generation voice) launches today in ChatGPT.

it feels magical and 'real'.

i have always preferred typing to talking to an AI, now i think that's going to shift.

takes.name: OpenAI
takes.text:
GPT-Live is now fully rolled out to all ChatGPT users on Go, Plus, and Pro plans. Free user rollout is in progress. 

Update to the latest version of the ChatGPT app on iOS or Android to try it out.

stories.title: Higgsfield Apps Lets Fable 5 Engineer Full Apps Around Its Video Models
stories.summary: Higgsfield launched Higgsfield Apps, which pairs its image/video generation models with Fable 5 acting as the engineer to build and ship full aesthetic web apps in one click, backed by a $100,000 build contest.

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Our biggest release yet. 
Introducing Higgsfield Apps.

1. Generate apps with any Higgsfield image or video model built in.
2. Fable 5 engineers every app the way a top engineer would.
3. Build aesthetic websites of any complexity.
4. Ship browser extensions, plugins, desktop and https://t.co/2RnfQ3tGK0

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
The $100,000 Higgsfield App Contest is on.

Build an app on Claude and deploy it in one click via Higgsfield MCP. 13 winners split the pool, with $25,000 for first place.

Submit by July 22. Winners announced July 29.

The time to launch your app is NOW. https://t.co/nsOvwS8cjd

hotTakes.name: spidey
hotTakes.text: "I'll just learn coding, get a remote job, and work from Bali." https://t.co/D63raJznfS

hotTakes.name: ThePrimeagen
hotTakes.text:
1 hour of hand programming

ngmi https://t.co/WTw7PIGnJg

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text:
someone at @SlackHQ please add a reply feature. i'm begging you

just copy discord DO NOT GET CREATIVE

please

hotTakes.name: shadcn
hotTakes.text: I still need a really good code editor. Are we going too fast in the other direction?

hotTakes.name: colinhacks
hotTakes.text: GitHub breaking https://t.co/yEjPT6KPAw is the most unforgivable thing yet https://t.co/gEy5QOtIKm

hotTakes.name: trash
hotTakes.text: unreal engine is the react of the gaming industry

hotTakes.name: mark
hotTakes.text: Hot take, we need no reasoning effort controls, the model should be able to figure out how much to think on its own.

hotTakes.name: Guillermo Rauch
hotTakes.text:
It’s model release week. I suspect Meta Spark 1.1, Grok 4.5, and GLM 5.2 will significantly displace token market share. 

Most agentic tasks require reasonably high intelligence at fast speeds.

It’s a good time to be using ▲ AI Gateway!
